title: ProdMan Compiled Spec
description: Machine-readable product spec generated by `prodman compile`. Structured for AI coding agent consumption.

feature.description: Kebab-case feature identifier. Matches the features/ folder name.

spec_version.description: Monotonically incrementing version. Bumped on each compile.

readiness.description: Agent-readiness state derived from linter results. 'agent-ready' means all required fields pass lint. 'review-needed' means warnings exist but no blockers. 'incomplete' means required sections are missing.

compiled_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this spec was compiled.

task_summary.description: 1–2 sentences. Exactly what the agent should build or change. Precise about outcome, not implementation.

product_context.description: High-level product and user context from prodman-context/.

affected_segment.description: Specific user segment(s) impacted by this feature from prodman-context/users.md.

technical_context.description: Technical grounding to orient the agent in the codebase.

relevant_files.description: Files and modules the agent should focus on.

related_systems.description: APIs, databases, or services this task touches.

problem.description: The user problem this feature solves. 2–4 sentences.

users.description: User segments this feature directly affects.

segment.description: Segment name. Must be specific — not 'users'.

description.description: Who they are and what they're trying to accomplish.

in_scope.description: Specific things to build. Each item should be independently verifiable.

out_of_scope.description: Explicit exclusions. Do not touch these. Prevents scope creep during implementation.

requirements.description: Must-implement requirements. Each must be specific and testable.

anti_requirements.description: Things the agent must NOT do. Explicit constraints on implementation approach.

constraints.description: Non-functional constraints the implementation must satisfy.

performance.description: Latency, throughput, or load requirements. Use 'None specified' if not applicable.

compliance.description: Legal, regulatory, or security constraints. Use 'None specified' if not applicable.

accessibility.description: WCAG level or specific accessibility requirements. Use 'None specified' if not applicable.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable conditions that must ALL be true for the task to be complete.

statement.description: Observable and testable. Starts with 'When' or a specific action.

testable.description: Must be true. Untestable criteria are invalid.

edge_cases.description: Scenarios the implementation must explicitly handle.

definition_of_done.description: Checklist of conditions the agent must satisfy before the task is considered complete.

escalation_triggers.description: Conditions under which the agent must stop and ask rather than decide. Empty array is valid only if the spec has zero ambiguous decision points.

context_refs.description: Paths to source files that were compiled into this spec.

tech_md.description: Path to prodman-context/tech.md (optional — generated by VS Code tech detector)

examples.task_summary: Add a keyword search field to the /orders page that filters the displayed order list by order number, item name, or seller name. Results update on each keystroke, debounced at 300ms. No new route — inline filtering on already-loaded data.

product_context.product: Mobile-first marketplace where buyers purchase from independent sellers and manage purchase history.
product_context.affected_segment: Mobile checkout repeat buyers (3+ orders/month) who need to locate specific past orders.

examples.problem: Mobile users cannot find a specific past order without scrolling their entire history. This causes 47 support tickets/week and confirmed repeat purchases users later return.

users.description: Users with 3+ orders who need to locate past orders for reordering, tracking, returns, or receipts.

in_scope: Search input above the order list on /orders
Client-side filtering on order_number, item_name, seller_name (case-insensitive, partial match)
Empty state when results are empty
out_of_scope: Do not modify GET /api/orders or any backend code
Do not persist search state across page refreshes
requirements: Search input renders above order list in OrdersPage.tsx
Filtering applied client-side using debounced value (300ms)
Filtering matches against order_number, item_name, seller_name (case-insensitive, partial match)
Empty state renders when results array is empty with copy including the query
Clear button renders when input has value, hidden when empty
anti_requirements: Do not introduce a new route or page for search results
Do not make additional API calls on each keystroke
Do not modify any component outside OrdersPage.tsx, OrderList.tsx, and new SearchInput.tsx

constraints.performance: Filter must complete within 16ms for lists up to 200 orders
constraints.accessibility: Search input must have aria-label='Search orders'. Clear button must have aria-label='Clear search'.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Search input renders on the Orders page, visually above the order list
Typing in the search field filters visible orders within 300ms (debounced)
Filtering matches on item_name: searching 'wool coat' shows only orders containing that item
Empty state renders when filtered results are empty; copy includes the current query
Clearing the input restores the complete unfiltered order list

edge_cases.expected: Empty state renders with query echoed in copy
Treat as empty — show full list, do not trigger filter
Skip that field in match; do not throw

definition_of_done: New tests written and passing: filtering logic, debounce, empty state, clear button
No regressions in src/pages/orders/ or src/components/orders/
Analytics events orders_search_query and orders_search_cleared instrumented
escalation_triggers: If Order type is missing item_name or seller_name fields, stop and ask PM for correct field names
If search persistence via URL param is requested, stop and confirm — this is explicitly out of scope
